name: Security, Governance, And External Sharing
principle: Protect the Microsoft 365 substrate before partner, client, or agentic access expands.

sources.title: Security defaults in Microsoft Entra ID
Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive

baseline.target: Security Defaults enabled now unless a Business Premium / Entra P1 Conditional Access plan replaces it.
baseline.notes: Security Defaults are free and strong, but they can block device-code auth. Before enabling, confirm the replacement auth pattern for setup scripts.

baseline.target: Two break-glass Global Admins, controlled admin account, and Adam's accepted-risk daily Global Admin reviewed at least monthly.
baseline.notes: Revisit just-in-time elevation after Business Premium or Entra P1 is available.

baseline.target: No casual broad-write app consent. Setup/provisioning apps are disabled, consent-revoked, or explicitly marked active only while needed.
baseline.notes: The idle agent-pnp-provisioning app remains a Stage 7 review item because it has broad delegated setup scopes.

baseline.target: No guests until a named partner/client onboarding decision is approved. Guest invitations should be admin/guest-inviter controlled, not everyone.
baseline.notes: Guest access is a business workflow, not a default convenience.

baseline.target: Tenant and sites remain as restrictive as practical. Client/partner sharing is enabled only for named sites with named domains or specific people.
baseline.notes: Stage 3 built sites with external sharing off; preserve that default until a client workspace pattern is approved.

baseline.target: Default to specific people or organization-only links. Avoid anonymous Anyone links for business records.
baseline.notes: Anyone links are a future exception path, not a default for an AI-first operating substrate.

baseline.target: Define a plain-language sensitivity/retention model now; implement Microsoft labels/retention when licensing supports it.
baseline.notes: The operating rule can exist before the Purview feature set is fully licensed.

baseline.target: Sync only active trusted libraries to Adam's machine. Client-owned or sensitive libraries stay browser-only unless approved.
baseline.notes: This protects future client work and keeps local sprawl under control.

baseline.target: Review sign-ins, directory role membership, guests, app consent, external sharing, and agent action logs on a written cadence.
baseline.notes: Monthly is enough for early stage; increase cadence when clients or partners are active.

baseline.target: Agents may read and propose inside scoped surfaces. External sends, guest invites, sharing changes, calendar commitments, and tenant policy changes require Adam approval.
baseline.notes: This preserves the AI-first posture without allowing uncontrolled agency.

stageExitCriteria: Read-only Stage 7 inventory captured and summarized.
Business Premium / Entra P1 decision recorded, including Founders Hub path.
Security Defaults versus Conditional Access path chosen.
External sharing default and named exception process documented.
Partner onboarding security gate defined before any real external invite.
No Stage 8 client pattern begins until Stage 7 high-risk gaps are accepted or closed.
